assumptions: All components run in the same Go process using goroutines and channels.
The task queue is an in-memory channel-based queue.
The results database is a simple file-based store (e.g., JSON file).
The API gateway exposes an HTTP endpoint for submitting tasks.
constraints: No external dependencies beyond the Go standard library.
All components must be in a single Go module.
The system must handle concurrent task submissions and processing.
success_criteria: Tasks are submitted via HTTP and processed asynchronously.
The system handles multiple concurrent tasks without data corruption.

test_cases.name: TestTaskSubmission
test_cases.description: Submit a task via the API gateway and verify it is queued.

test_cases.name: TestWorkerProcessing
test_cases.description: Verify the worker processes a task from the queue and stores the result.

test_cases.name: TestConcurrentTasks
test_cases.description: Submit multiple tasks concurrently and verify all are processed.
